quotes.text: A lot of times, my swing feels completely lost on the range. You wake up, the body is stiff, the hands feel totally disconnected, and you just have to hit knockdown wedges until you find your window again. You literally have to dig it out of the dirt.
quotes.speaker: Tiger Woods
quotes.context: Pre-tournament media availability at the Masters
quotes.fake_note: Tiger loves the Ben Hogan 'dig it out of the dirt' reference and talks about 'windows' for ball flight, but admitting his swing feels 'completely lost' before a round is a vulnerability he rarely shows to the press.

quotes.text: We're not building a time capsule here. It's week six. Nobody cares what we did last Sunday. We had poor pad level, we missed tackles in space, and our communication on punt return was unacceptable. We've got a lot of ground to cover before Wednesday.
quotes.speaker: Bill Belichick
quotes.context: Post-game press conference after a sloppy win
quotes.fake_note: It's a perfect 'greatest hits' of his dismissive presser clichés, but the 'time capsule' phrase is an invented variation of his actual, famous 'we're not building a watch' quote.

quotes.text: I think people underestimate how much of tennis is just managing your own frustration. You miss a shot you've hit a thousand times in practice, and you have about five seconds to forgive yourself before the next serve. If you hold onto it, the match is gone.
quotes.speaker: Serena Williams
quotes.fake_note: It sounds exactly like her elite competitive mindset, but the rigid 'five seconds to forgive yourself' is a generalized sports psychology trope rather than her specific documented process.
